title: China Economy - South China Morning Post
description: China economy news, including budget, the stock market and analysis.

items.title: Tech giant ASML joins China trade trip even amid US risk to sales
items.creator: Brian Rhoads,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Tech giant ASML joins China trade trip even amid US risk to sales
items.content:
Executives from Dutch technology giants ASML and NXP will join a trade mission to China next month, as the Netherlands seeks deals with the Asian nation while also contending with US efforts to pare its hi-tech exports to the country.
In total, 17 officials from Dutch companies will join Trade Minister Sjoerd Sjoerdsma on the trip to Beijing, the South China Morning Post reported on Wednesday, citing an unidentified person. Executives agreed to make the trip on condition that Sjoerdsma doesn’t...

items.title: Nomura economist on China’s deflation woes, fraught Japan ties
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Nomura economist on China’s deflation woes, fraught Japan ties
items.content:
Nomura Research Institute chief economist Richard Koo is well-known for his “balance sheet recession” interpretation of Japan’s economic downfall in the 1990s. In his second Open Questions interview, Koo assesses the risks the Chinese economy is facing this year and beyond, the many parallels between Beijing and Tokyo, and why market size and technology alone may not be enough to help avert a fate similar to Japan’s.
Koo also explains why giving China’s consumers money will not help and why...

items.title: China-Japan flights to drop 57% in summer travel peak amid geopolitical tensions
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China-Japan flights to drop 57% in summer travel peak amid geopolitical tensions
items.content:
The number of China-Japan flights scheduled by airlines for the coming summer travel period – the peak months of July and August – has dropped by 57 per cent versus a year ago following a political flap and a price hike in Japanese visas, according to industry data.
A total of 2,629 round-trip flights were expected next month between the two Asian countries, down from 6,317 in July last year. In August, 2,641 were scheduled, a drop from the 6,127 flown in August 2025, data compiled by British...

items.title: Is EV battery technology nearing an inflection point? CATL boss expresses doubts
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Is EV battery technology nearing an inflection point? CATL boss expresses doubts
items.content:
Robin Zeng Yuqun, head of the world’s largest electric vehicle (EV) battery maker, has played down expectations for the much-hyped solid-state battery technology, predicting an inflection point would not take place until 2030.
The founder and chairman of Contemporary Amperex Technology Ltd (CATL) also warned on Tuesday that commercial viability of the next-generation batteries was yet to be established.
“Based on a measure of level one to nine, the technology has only reached level four so far,”...

items.title: A tale of two cities: can Shanghai challenge Hong Kong’s offshore finance crown?
items.creator: Daisy Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: A tale of two cities: can Shanghai challenge Hong Kong’s offshore finance crown?
items.content:
Hong Kong has been seizing every opportunity to consolidate its role as a global financial centre, leveraging unique advantages and national strategies. In the first piece of our miniseries focusing on the city’s financial industry ahead of the handover anniversary, we examine what Shanghai’s rapid development of offshore markets means to Hong Kong.
Beijing’s latest push to strengthen Shanghai’s offshore financial capabilities is prompting fresh questions about whether Hong Kong’s long-standing...

items.title: Record-breaking bridges in eastern China to boost port and railway connections
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Record-breaking bridges in eastern China to boost port and railway connections
items.content:
China has completed the main span of a record-breaking sea bridge at Ningbo-Zhoushan port, with the new link expected to improve the efficiency of logistics at the world’s busiest cargo port.
The main structure of the 2,212-metre (7,257-foot) Qinglongmen Grand Bridge in eastern China’s Zhejiang province has been completed, Science and Technology Daily reported on Tuesday, adding that it would link a cluster of islands within the Ningbo-Zhoushan port complex.
The bridge is the world’s longest...

items.title: Why the Vietnam ‘detour’ for US-bound Chinese goods hit a dead end
items.creator: Frank Chen,Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why the Vietnam ‘detour’ for US-bound Chinese goods hit a dead end
items.content:
Nearly a decade ago, one of China’s largest textile exporters concocted a plan to hedge against trade barriers championed by US President Donald Trump during his first term.
Its owners were convinced that moving their primary production base from China’s east coast to Vietnam would be a permanent workaround – bypassing complex supply-chain pitfalls by hunkering down in a tariff lowland and continuing to stock Walmart shelves with socks and towels.
Trying to get the jump on Trump in 2017, from...

items.title: China offers rewards for reporting rare earth export control violations
items.creator: Xiaofei Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China offers rewards for reporting rare earth export control violations
items.content:
Beijing is sharpening the tools it uses to police rare earth and other strategic mineral exports, announcing fresh measures that will reward companies and individuals for reporting suspected violations.
The announcement came on Wednesday, the same day Tokyo confirmed that two of its nationals had been detained in China over alleged attempts to smuggle rare earth-related goods out of the country.
“Any organisation or individual has the right to report conduct suspected of violating relevant laws...

items.title: China’s Xi joins leadership blitz of hub visits in fact-finding push before GDP release
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s Xi joins leadership blitz of hub visits in fact-finding push before GDP release
items.content:
China’s top leadership launched an expansive sweep of the nation’s agricultural, industrial and tech hubs this week, gathering “frontline” data ahead of a crucial GDP release in mid-July and a high-stakes Politburo meeting at the month’s end.
On Wednesday, President Xi Jinping conducted a field trip to Dezhou, an agrarian city in the northern Shandong province, where he visited cornfields and a village. During the tour, he highlighted the need to enhance production and yield to guarantee food...

items.title: Hong Kong readies offshore yuan initiatives after Shanghai move
items.creator: Brian Rhoads,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Hong Kong readies offshore yuan initiatives after Shanghai move
items.content:
Hong Kong’s finance chief says the city is planning new measures to boost offshore yuan trading, days after mainland Chinese authorities took steps to spur such trading in Shanghai.
The initiatives will be announced next month, Financial Secretary Paul Chan Mo-po said in an interview with the South China Morning Post published on Monday. He declined to elaborate on the new measures.
Hong Kong also wants to see more companies offering yuan-denominated shares on the city’s stock exchange, Chan...

items.title: China’s central bank sets weaker yuan midpoint against US dollar for third day
items.creator: Julie Zhang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s central bank sets weaker yuan midpoint against US dollar for third day
items.content:
Chinese authorities have weakened the yuan’s midpoint against the US dollar for a third trading day, despite the European Central Bank chief joining a chorus highlighting the supposed undervaluation of the Chinese currency.
The People’s Bank of China set the yuan’s midpoint rate – which guides onshore market trading within a 2 per cent band on the up and down sides – at 6.8195 per US dollar, compared with 6.8171 on Tuesday.
A three-year high of 6.8088 was set on June 15, buoyed by the easing of...

items.title: ‘China Opportunity 2.0’: Li Qiang calls for revisal of Western narrative at Summer Davos (原标题: ‘China Opportunity 2.0’: Li Qiang calls for revisal of Western narrative at ‘Summer Davos’)
items.creator: Ji Siqi,Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: ‘China Opportunity 2.0’: Li Qiang calls for revisal of Western narrative at Summer Davos
items.content:
Chinese Premier Li Qiang said on Wednesday the western narrative of “China Shock 2.0” is not grounded in facts and instead should be phrased as “China Opportunity 2.0”, as he stressed the country is serving as a catalyst for global economic growth.
“What China’s technologies and products in emerging fields bring to the world is not a shock, but an opportunity; not a threat, but empowerment,” Li said at the opening plenary of the World Economic Forum’s Annual Meeting of the New Champions, also...

items.title: China, Kazakhstan target deeper ties in minerals and energy at Summer Davos (原标题: China, Kazakhstan target deeper ties in minerals and energy at ‘Summer Davos’)

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China, Kazakhstan target deeper ties in minerals and energy at Summer Davos
items.content:
China and Kazakhstan have reiterated a desire for closer cooperation in energy, with both sides seeking to build on Belt and Road Initiative projects that have turned the Central Asian nation into a key transit hub between China and Europe.
In a meeting between Premier Li Qiang and Kazakh Prime Minister Olzhas Bektenov in Dalian, Liaoning province, on Tuesday, Beijing expressed its willingness to work with Astana on deepening cooperation in energy and mineral resources, Xinhua...

items.title: What fuels global trade imbalances: China’s overcapacity or America’s failure to adapt?
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: What fuels global trade imbalances: China’s overcapacity or America’s failure to adapt?
items.content:
China’s industrial overcapacity should not be singled out for blame amid trade imbalances, as economies worldwide are struggling to adapt to shifts in the global economy, contributing to mounting pressures, a prominent Chinese scholar said in comments directed at the United States.
“The reason why our imbalance is becoming a bigger problem is that the rest of the world is having a bigger problem in adjusting the economic structure,” said Huang Yiping, dean of Peking University’s National School...

items.title: China’s trading crackdown seen boosting Hong Kong as official capital hub
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s trading crackdown seen boosting Hong Kong as official capital hub
items.content:
China’s crackdown on cross-border securities trading could strengthen – rather than diminish – Hong Kong’s financial role, according to economists, as Beijing steers more capital through official channels and reinforces the city’s status as an offshore yuan hub.
“I would argue that these moves make the importance of Hong Kong even bigger,” Diana Choyleva, founder and chief economist at Enodo Economics, said on Tuesday at the World Economic Forum’s Annual Meeting of the New Champions – also known...

items.title: Anxious West seeks to rewrite global trade rules to counter China
items.creator: Wang Huiyao
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Anxious West seeks to rewrite global trade rules to counter China
items.content:
At the recent Brussels summit, the leaders of all 27 European Union states called on the European Commission to expand the bloc’s trade defence toolbox against “global macroeconomic imbalances”, widely understood to refer to China’s so-called overcapacity.
Measures under discussion include mechanisms for sector-wide tariffs and other restrictions in industries such as chemicals and green technology. Europe, long a critic of Washington’s Section 301 tariffs, is now considering instruments that...

items.title: Global farming, Chinese food imports at stake as El Nino threatens drought
items.creator: Ralph Jennings,Mia Nurmamat
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Global farming, Chinese food imports at stake as El Nino threatens drought
items.content:
A dry, hot El Nino climate pattern is threatening agriculture in much of the world, the United Nations’ Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said, while an analyst warned of a possible challenge to China’s food supplies.
The “sharpest” El Nino risks would occur in parts of Southeast Asia, South Asia, Africa and Latin America, according to the FAO, which noted their pastureland faced more than a 50 per cent chance of drought over the coming months.
El Nino cycles can destroy harvests, kill...

items.title: China targets US rare earth miners as Pentagon faces restocking rush
items.creator: Brian Rhoads,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China targets US rare earth miners as Pentagon faces restocking rush
items.content:
China put two US rare earth miners on its export control list, along with eight technology companies, in response to the US Department of Defence blacklisting Chinese businesses for their alleged ties to the People’s Liberation Army.
MP Materials and USA Rare Earth will both be banned from buying Chinese exports with dual civilian and military uses, the Ministry of Commerce said on Monday. Motor maker Aveox and drone companies Red Cat and Teal Drones were among the other companies included in...

items.title: China’s evolving political economy, as seen through gaokao scramble
items.creator: Winston Mok
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s evolving political economy, as seen through gaokao scramble
items.content:
This week, the results of China’s National Higher Education Entrance Examination, or gaokao, will be released. Within days, candidates will submit their prioritised preferences for universities and majors. Families will have to make the most consequential choices with imperfect information amid uncertainty.
It is a sorting mechanism. Candidates need to find the right match by choosing programmes their gaokao results can gain them entry to. Historical admission marks only serve as indicative...
